2605.22579.title: Beyond Temperature: Hyperfitting as a Late-Stage Geometric Expansion
2605.22579.paper_summary: Recent work has identified a counterintuitive phenomenon termed "Hyperfitting", where fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) to near-zero training loss on small datasets surprisingly enhances open-ended generation quality and mitigates repetition in greedy decoding. While effective, the underlying mechanism remains poorly understood, with the extremely low-entropy output distributions suggesting a potential equivalence to simple temperature scaling. In this work, we demonstrate that this phenomenon is fundamentally distinct from distribution sharpening; entropy-matched control experiments reveal that temperature scaling fails to replicate the diversity gains of hyperfitting. Furthermore, we falsify the hypothesis of static vocabulary reweighting, showing through ablation studies that hyperfitting relies on a dynamic, context-dependent rank reordering mechanism. Layer-wise analysis localizes this effect to a "Terminal Expansion" in the final transformer block, where a substantial geometric expansion of the feature space (Delta Dim approx +80.8) facilitates the promotion of deep-tail tokens. Additionally, we introduce Late-Stage LoRA, a targeted fine-tuning strategy that updates only the final 5 layers, yielding robust generation with minimal parameter updates
2605.22579.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究已識別出一種反直覺的現象，稱為「超擬合」，即在小型數據集上對大型語言模型（LLMs）進行微調以接近零的訓練損失，驚人地提高了開放式生成質量並減少了貪婪解碼中的重複。雖然這種方法有效，但其背後的機制仍然不甚明瞭，極低的熵輸出分佈暗示著與簡單的溫度縮放之間可能存在等價性。在本研究中，我們證明這一現象與分佈加強根本不同；熵匹配的對照實驗顯示，溫度縮放無法複製超擬合的多樣性增益。此外，我們駁斥了靜態詞彙重加權的假設，通過消融研究顯示，超擬合依賴於一種動態、上下文依賴的排名重排序機制。層級分析將這一效應定位於最終Transformer塊中的「終端擴展」，在這裡，特徵空間的顯著幾何擴展（Delta Dim 約 +80.8）促進了深尾標記的提升。此外，我們引入了晚期 LoRA，一種針對性的微調策略，只更新最後 5 層，從而以最小的參數更新實現穩健的生成。
2605.22579.author: Meimingwei Li et.al.
2605.22579.authors: Meimingwei Li, Yuanhao Ding, Esteban Garces Arias, Christian Heumann
